notes: If this check is failing, you must update to a supported version of the operating system.

The product will not be controlling patch levels of every package installed on user's systems upgrading every package installed on a system without testing could be detrimental to the health of the system.

Turning fips mode on in a machine that isn't already configured for it will cause connection issues to anything not configured to work in fips mode. It is more likely that the user will lose access to their systems or important services than it working out of the box.
The product will not automatically encrypt partitions. It is not possible to do this without understanding of the existing partition structure on the system.
The product cannot make assumptions about the system's pki infrastructure and will not install trust chains automatically.
The product cannot make assumptions about the user's pki certificate structure and will not make assumptions about which certificates are being used and where.
Locking a user due to inappropriate encryption is not currently available in simp. A mechanism to address this rule will be implemented in a future release.

The grub password is set via the password_pbkdf2 parameter, however, the rule explicitly checks for GRUB2_PASSWORD. The grub username and password are being set which meets the requirement of the rule, but the check for the rule is not checking for all possible methods of setting a password for a user.

The product parses the output of 'sshd -T' for the key that the system uses for communication and ensures that key is appropriately protected. The product will set pam to lock a user's account after 3 failed login attempts in a 15 minute period, however, there will also be some extra options in the pam sections this rule requests remediation for. This check will fail if there is a commented out line that looks like "#PrintLastLog = <value>" even if the correct value of "PrintLastLog = true" exists in the /etc/ssh/sshd_config file.

There is no reliable way to remove extraneous umask commands without causing other errors. However, the umask that the product sets should take precedence over any others in the file since it will be located at the bottom.

The product will set the mode of the files referenced in this rule to "700" and "600" rather then "750" and "640". This will lock down the files and keep them secure so only admins and ISSMs can access them.
